About Me

Engineer. Builder. Innovator.

I am Evans Sabvure, a 2026 Aeronautical Engineering (Avionics) graduate from the University of Zimbabwe. I am passionate about robotics and automation — specifically aerial robotics — using the Robot Operating System (ROS).

I specialise in UAV software development, embedded systems, and intelligent autonomous systems powered by machine learning. From building solar-electric VTOL drones to programming farm robots that won medals at international competitions, I turn ideas into flying, moving, thinking machines.

Currently serving as Vice President of both the UZ Robotics Club and UZ Drone Club, leading teams to international competitions and mentoring the next generation of Zimbabwean engineers.

Projects

What I've Built

Project 01

Chisiyamhepo

Aug 2025 — Present ● Active

A Solar-Electric Vertical Take-Off and Landing (VTOL) Fixed-Wing drone designed for medical supply transportation across remote areas of Zimbabwe and security surveillance of critical infrastructure.

I developed the UAV monitoring ground control UI using Python and Qt, the autonomous navigation software, and a pose-driven ML model for human action recognition — detecting suspicious activity such as climbing at pole-mounted transformer sites and automatically triggering drone surveillance response.

PythonQtMAVLinkArduPilotPose MLVTOLSolar-Electric
Project 02

CHIZADZAMATURA

Aug 2025 — Present ● Active

A multipurpose agricultural drone for Zimbabwean farmers integrating precision spraying, farm security surveillance, and cattle tracking in a single platform.

I developed the autonomous navigation stack using MAVLink and an ML algorithm for crop disease detection that guides precision chemical application, reducing waste and improving yield.

MAVLinkPythonArduPilotCrop Disease MLPrecision SprayingMulti-Rotor
Project 03

Runway FOD Detection UAV

May 2025

An intelligent multi-rotor UAV system for autonomous detection and mitigation of Foreign Object Debris (FOD) on airport runways, enhancing airside safety and reducing risk to aircraft.

The system uses a YOLOv8 model trained to detect metallic debris and loose rocks, running on a Raspberry Pi companion computer. Gazebo simulation was used for algorithm validation before real-world deployment.

YOLOv8Computer VisionRaspberry PiGazeboROSEmbedded C++
Project 04

Pallet Stacking & Handling Robot

May 2024  ·  IEEE ICRA Bronze Medal

An autonomous robot for stacking and handling pallets for Japanese Bento preparation, demonstrated at the IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) Bots & Bento Competition — earning a Bronze Medal.

Used SLAM algorithms, point cloud to laser scan conversion, and autonomous path planning with Python and ROS. The robot used Mecanum wheels for omnidirectional movement in tight competition spaces.

SLAMROSNav2PythonPoint Cloud → LaserScanMecanum Wheels
Project 05

Autonomous Farm Navigation Robot

July 2023  ·  PARC Silver Medal

A multipurpose mobile robot for autonomous farm navigation, crop yield estimation, weed detection & removal, and precision insecticide application. Awarded Silver Medal at the Pan African Robotics Competition Engineers League 2023.

Used SLAM algorithms, point cloud to laser scan conversion, and autonomous path planning with Python and ROS — with separate simulation and real-world demonstration phases.

SLAMROSNav2PythonWeed Detection MLObstacle AvoidancePoint Cloud
Project 06

Arduino-Based Quadcopter

June 2025

Designed and built a quadcopter from scratch using Arduino as the flight controller, including full sensor integration, PID tuning, ESC calibration, and airframe construction.

ArduinoC++PID TuningIMUESC CalibrationEmbedded Systems
